title: Attention Is All You Need
abstract: The dominant sequence transduction models are based on complex recurrent or convolutional neural networks. We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms.

title: Generative Adversarial Nets
abstract: We propose a new framework for estimating generative models via an adversarial process, in which we simultaneously train two models.

title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision
abstract: We demonstrate that the simple pre-training task of predicting which caption goes with which image is an efficient and scalable way to learn state-of-the-art image representations (CLIP).

title: ImageNet: A Large-Scale Hierarchical Image Database
abstract: We introduce ImageNet, a large-scale ontology of images built upon the backbone of the WordNet structure.

title: Highly accurate protein structure prediction with AlphaFold
abstract: Proteins are essential to life and understanding their structure can facilitate a mechanistic understanding of their function.

title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
abstract: We demonstrate that scaling up language models greatly improves task-agnostic, few-shot performance.

title: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
abstract: We show an avenue for aligning language models with user intent on a wide range of tasks by fine-tuning with human feedback.

title: Gradient-based learning applied to document recognition
abstract: Multilayer neural networks trained with the back-propagation algorithm constitute the best example of a successful gradient-based learning technique.

title: Holistic Evaluation of Language Models
abstract: We present Holistic Evaluation of Language Models (HELM) to improve the transparency of language models.

title: A Path Towards Autonomous Machine Intelligence
abstract: How could machines learn as efficiently as humans and animals? How could machines learn to reason and plan?

title: Liquid Time-constant Networks
abstract: We introduce a new class of time-continuous recurrent neural network models. Instead of declaring a learning system's dynamics by implicit nonlinearities, we construct networks of linear first-order dynamical systems modulated via nonlinear interlinked gates.

title: The Lottery Ticket Hypothesis: Finding Sparse, Trainable Neural Networks
abstract: We find that a standard pruning technique naturally uncovers subnetworks whose initializations made them capable of training effectively. We call these subnetworks winning lottery tickets.

title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models
abstract: We explore how generating a chain of thought — a series of intermediate reasoning steps — significantly improves the ability of large language models to perform complex reasoning.

title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
abstract: We introduce BERT, a new language representation model that pre-trains deep bidirectional representations from unlabeled text by jointly conditioning on both left and right context in all layers.

title: Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition
abstract: We present a residual learning framework to ease the training of networks that are substantially deeper than those used previously. Residual networks are easier to optimize and gain accuracy from considerably increased depth.

title: ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks
abstract: We trained a large, deep convolutional neural network to classify the 1.2 million high-resolution images in the ImageNet LSVRC-2010 contest into 1000 different classes, achieving a top-5 test error rate of 15.3%.

title: Hierarchical Text-Conditional Image Generation with CLIP Latents
abstract: We propose a two-stage model: a prior that generates a CLIP image embedding given a text caption, and a decoder that generates an image conditioned on the image embedding (DALL-E 2).

title: High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models
abstract: We apply diffusion models in the latent space of powerful pretrained autoencoders, achieving state-of-the-art image synthesis while reducing computational costs. This work underlies Stable Diffusion.

title: Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning for Fast Adaptation of Deep Networks
abstract: We propose an algorithm for meta-learning that is model-agnostic and trains the model's initial parameters such that a small number of gradient steps with a small amount of training data produces good generalization on a new task.

title: Mastering the game of Go with deep neural networks and tree search
abstract: We introduce a new approach to computer Go that uses value networks to evaluate board positions and policy networks to select moves, combined with a Monte Carlo tree search. AlphaGo defeated the human European Go champion.

title: GPT-4 Technical Report
abstract: We report the development of GPT-4, a large-scale multimodal model which accepts image and text inputs and produces text outputs, exhibiting human-level performance on various professional and academic benchmarks.

title: Llama 2: Open Foundation and Fine-Tuned Chat Models
abstract: We develop and release Llama 2, a collection of pretrained and fine-tuned large language models ranging from 7B to 70B parameters, optimized for dialogue use cases and outperforming existing open-source chat models.

title: Long Short-Term Memory
abstract: We introduce Long Short-Term Memory, a novel recurrent network architecture with a gradient-based learning algorithm that can bridge minimal time lags in excess of 1000 discrete time steps, solving the vanishing gradient problem.

title: On the Dangers of Stochastic Parrots: Can Language Models Be Too Big?
abstract: We discuss the environmental and financial costs of training large language models, the inscrutability of their training data, and the potential for generating text that encodes hegemonic worldviews.

title: Rich feature hierarchies for accurate object detection and semantic segmentation
abstract: We propose a simple and scalable detection algorithm (R-CNN) that improves mean average precision by more than 30% relative to the previous best result on PASCAL VOC, by applying high-capacity CNNs to bottom-up region proposals.

title: Image-to-Image Translation with Conditional Adversarial Networks
abstract: We investigate conditional adversarial networks as a general-purpose solution to image-to-image translation problems (pix2pix).

title: Data-Centric Artificial Intelligence: A Survey
abstract: This survey provides a comprehensive overview of data-centric AI, including training data development, inference data development, and data maintenance.

title: Towards Deep Learning Models Resistant to Adversarial Attacks
abstract: We propose PGD adversarial training, a method for building robust deep learning models that resist adversarial perturbations.

title: A Generative Model for Molecular Distance Geometry
abstract: We present a generative model for 3D molecular geometry that enables drug discovery by predicting molecular conformations.

title: You Only Look Once: Unified, Real-Time Object Detection
abstract: We frame object detection as a regression problem to spatially separated bounding boxes and associated class probabilities. A single neural network predicts bounding boxes and class probabilities directly from full images in one evaluation.

title: OLMo: Accelerating the Science of Language Models
abstract: We present OLMo, a truly open language model with open training data, training code, model weights, and evaluation code. OLMo enables the scientific study of language models by making every artifact fully reproducible.

title: COMET: Commonsense Transformers for Automatic Knowledge Graph Construction
abstract: We present COMET, a framework for automatic construction of commonsense knowledge graphs by training transformers on existing knowledge tuples.

title: Deep contextualized word representations
abstract: We introduce a new type of deep contextualized word representation (ELMo) that models both complex characteristics of word use and how these uses vary across linguistic contexts.

title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
abstract: We demonstrate that scaling up language models greatly improves task-agnostic, few-shot performance, achieving strong results on many NLP benchmarks without fine-tuning (GPT-3).

title: Very Deep Convolutional Networks for Large-Scale Image Recognition
abstract: We investigate the effect of convolutional network depth on accuracy in large-scale image recognition, showing that pushing depth to 16-19 layers achieves significant improvement (VGGNet).

title: Efficient Estimation of Word Representations in Vector Space
abstract: We propose two novel model architectures for computing continuous vector representations of words from very large data sets, known as Word2Vec.

title: Auto-Encoding Variational Bayes
abstract: We introduce a stochastic variational inference and learning algorithm that scales to large datasets, enabling efficient approximate inference in directed probabilistic models (VAE).

title: Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization
abstract: We introduce Adam, an algorithm for first-order gradient-based optimization of stochastic objective functions that combines adaptive learning rates with momentum.

title: Reinforcement Learning: An Introduction
abstract: A comprehensive textbook providing a clear and simple account of the key ideas and algorithms of reinforcement learning, now considered the foundational reference in the field.

title: Semi-Supervised Classification with Graph Convolutional Networks
abstract: We present a scalable approach for semi-supervised learning on graph-structured data based on an efficient variant of convolutional neural networks operating directly on graphs (GCN).

title: Graph Attention Networks
abstract: We present graph attention networks (GATs), novel neural network architectures that operate on graph-structured data by leveraging masked self-attentional layers.

title: Learning Phrase Representations using RNN Encoder-Decoder for Statistical Machine Translation
abstract: We propose a novel neural network model called RNN Encoder-Decoder that learns to encode a variable-length sequence into a fixed-length vector and decode it back, introducing the GRU unit.

title: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
abstract: We discuss five practical research problems related to accident risk in machine learning systems, covering avoiding negative side effects, reward hacking, scalable oversight, safe exploration, and distributional shift.

title: DeepLab: Semantic Image Segmentation with Deep Convolutional Nets, Atrous Convolution, and Fully Connected CRFs
abstract: We propose DeepLab, a system that uses atrous convolution and fully connected CRFs to achieve state-of-the-art semantic image segmentation.

title: Pyramid Scene Parsing Network
abstract: We propose a pyramid pooling module that exploits global context information for scene parsing, achieving state-of-the-art results on multiple benchmarks (PSPNet).

title: Aggregated Residual Transformations for Deep Neural Networks
abstract: We present ResNeXt, a simple and highly modularized network architecture for image classification that uses a homogeneous multi-branch architecture with aggregated transformations.

title: Deep Compression: Compressing Deep Neural Networks with Pruning, Trained Quantization and Huffman Coding
abstract: We introduce a three-stage pipeline for compressing neural networks: pruning, quantization, and Huffman coding, achieving 35-49x compression without loss of accuracy.

title: Geometric Deep Learning: Going Beyond Euclidean Data
abstract: We provide a unified framework for applying deep learning techniques to non-Euclidean domains such as graphs and manifolds, establishing the foundations of geometric deep learning.

title: Dropout as a Bayesian Approximation: Representing Model Uncertainty in Deep Learning
abstract: We show that dropout training in deep neural networks can be interpreted as approximate Bayesian inference, providing a practical way to obtain model uncertainty estimates.

title: Representation Learning on Graphs: Methods and Applications
abstract: We review methods for learning low-dimensional node embeddings on graphs, covering approaches based on matrix factorization, random walks, and graph neural networks.

title: Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach
abstract: The definitive textbook on artificial intelligence, covering search, knowledge representation, planning, learning, natural language processing, perception, and robotics.

title: Causal Inference for Statistics, Social, and Biomedical Sciences
abstract: A comprehensive introduction to causal inference using the potential outcomes framework, covering experimental design, matching, instrumental variables, and regression discontinuity.

title: Large-scale Video Classification with Convolutional Neural Networks
abstract: We study multiple approaches for extending CNN architectures for video classification, introducing the Sports-1M benchmark with 1 million YouTube videos across 487 classes.

title: Neural Architecture Search with Reinforcement Learning
abstract: We use a recurrent neural network trained with reinforcement learning to generate neural network architectures, discovering models that rival the best human-designed architectures.

title: Action Recognition with Trajectory-Pooled Deep-Convolutional Descriptors
abstract: We propose trajectory-pooled deep-convolutional descriptors (TDD) that combine improved dense trajectories with deep convolutional features for video action recognition.

title: Multi-Task Learning Using Uncertainty to Weigh Losses for Scene Geometry and Semantics
abstract: We propose a principled approach to multi-task learning using homoscedastic uncertainty to weigh multiple loss functions, demonstrating improvements on scene understanding tasks.

title: Towards Interpretable Machine Learning: A Survey on Methods and Metrics
abstract: We provide a comprehensive survey of interpretable machine learning methods including feature attribution, concept-based explanations, and model-agnostic approaches.

title: PointFlow: 3D Point Cloud Generation with Continuous Normalizing Flows
abstract: We propose PointFlow, a principled probabilistic framework for 3D point cloud generation using continuous normalizing flows that models the distribution of shapes as a two-level hierarchy.

title: SoK: Eternal War in Memory
abstract: A systematization of knowledge paper that categorizes memory corruption attacks and defenses, providing a comprehensive framework for understanding software security.

title: PointNet++: Deep Hierarchical Feature Learning on Point Sets in a Metric Space
abstract: We introduce a hierarchical neural network that recursively applies PointNet on nested partitions of the input point set, capturing local structures at multiple scales for 3D understanding.

title: Memory Networks
abstract: We describe a new class of learning models called memory networks that combine inference with a long-term memory component for question answering and language understanding tasks.

title: GradNorm: Gradient Normalization for Adaptive Loss Balancing in Deep Multitask Networks
abstract: We present GradNorm, an algorithm that automatically balances training in deep multitask models by dynamically tuning gradient magnitudes.

title: Variational Graph Auto-Encoders
abstract: We introduce the variational graph auto-encoder (VGAE), a framework for unsupervised learning on graph-structured data based on the variational auto-encoder.

title: Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition
abstract: We present a residual learning framework to ease the training of networks that are substantially deeper than those used previously, enabling 152-layer deep networks on ImageNet.

title: Long Short-Term Memory
abstract: We introduce a novel recurrent network architecture called Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) that can learn to bridge minimal time lags in excess of 1000 discrete time steps.

title: SSD: Single Shot MultiBox Detector
abstract: We present a method for detecting objects in images using a single deep neural network that discretizes the output space of bounding boxes into a set of default boxes over different aspect ratios and scales.

title: Places: A 10 Million Image Database for Scene Recognition
abstract: We introduce the Places Database containing over 10 million images comprising 400+ unique scene categories, enabling large-scale visual understanding of scenes.

title: DeepLab: Semantic Image Segmentation with Deep Convolutional Nets and Fully Connected CRFs
abstract: We combine deep convolutional neural networks with fully connected conditional random fields to produce semantically accurate and detailed segmentation of images.

title: Visual Object Tracking using Adaptive Correlation Filters
abstract: We propose a tracking framework based on adaptive correlation filters trained with a regularized least squares approach, achieving real-time performance on visual object tracking benchmarks.

title: Learning to Rank: From Pairwise Approach to Listwise Approach
abstract: We propose a listwise approach to learning to rank that directly optimizes a listwise loss function defined on the predicted permutation distribution of documents.

title: Measuring Massive Multitask Language Understanding
abstract: We propose a test covering 57 subjects across STEM, humanities, and social sciences to measure a language model's multitask accuracy, which we call MMLU.

title: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models
abstract: We present high quality image synthesis results using diffusion probabilistic models, showing that these models can produce samples rivaling the best GAN results.

title: Equality of Opportunity in Supervised Learning
abstract: We propose a framework for fair supervised learning where the goal is to ensure that the protected attribute is independent of the prediction conditional on the true label.

title: Reading Wikipedia to Answer Open-Domain Questions
abstract: We propose DrQA, a system for reading comprehension applied to open-domain question answering at the scale of Wikipedia, combining document retrieval with a neural reader.

title: Accurate, Large Minibatch SGD: Training ImageNet in 1 Hour
abstract: We present techniques for efficient large-scale distributed training using large minibatch sizes, training ResNet-50 on ImageNet in one hour with 256 GPUs.

title: A Reduction of Imitation Learning and Structured Prediction to No-Regret Online Learning
abstract: We propose DAgger, an iterative algorithm that trains a policy under its own induced distribution of states, achieving strong theoretical guarantees for imitation learning.

title: Language Models as Knowledge Bases?
abstract: We analyze the factual knowledge stored in pretrained language models by probing them with cloze-style queries, finding that BERT contains surprisingly rich relational knowledge.

title: Covariate Shift Adaptation by Importance Weighted Cross Validation
abstract: We propose a method for adapting classifiers under covariate shift using importance-weighted cross validation to select model parameters without labeled target samples.

title: Introduction to Online Convex Optimization
abstract: We present a comprehensive survey of online convex optimization covering fundamental algorithms, regret bounds, and applications to machine learning.

title: A Simple Framework for Contrastive Learning of Visual Representations
abstract: We present SimCLR, a simple framework for contrastive learning of visual representations that outperforms previous self-supervised and semi-supervised methods on ImageNet.

title: Reconciling Modern Machine Learning Practice and the Bias-Variance Trade-off
abstract: We demonstrate the double descent phenomenon, showing that increasing model complexity beyond the interpolation threshold can lead to improved test performance, challenging classical bias-variance intuitions.

title: OGB-LSC: A Large-Scale Challenge for Machine Learning on Graphs
abstract: We present the Open Graph Benchmark Large-Scale Challenge, providing large-scale graph datasets for node classification, link prediction, and graph regression.

title: Speech and Language Processing
abstract: A comprehensive textbook covering foundational concepts in NLP and speech processing, from n-gram language models to modern transformer architectures and large language models.

title: Explaining Nonlinear Classification Decisions with Deep Taylor Decomposition
abstract: We introduce deep Taylor decomposition, a method for explaining individual classification decisions by backpropagating relevance from the output to the input pixels.

title: SayCan: Grounding Language in Robotic Affordances
abstract: We present SayCan, which combines large language models with robotic affordance functions to ground language instructions in physically feasible robotic actions.

title: Language Through a Prism: A Spectral Approach for Multiscale Language Representations
abstract: We propose a spectral approach that decomposes language representations at different scales, capturing both local syntax and global semantics in a unified framework.

title: AMiner: Mining Deep Knowledge from Big Scholar Data
abstract: We present AMiner, a system for mining academic social networks that provides services including researcher profiling, expert finding, and topic modeling over millions of papers.

title: CPM: A Large-scale Generative Chinese Pre-trained Language Model
abstract: We release CPM, a generative Chinese pre-trained language model with 2.6 billion parameters, trained on 100GB of Chinese text data.

title: Sentence-BERT: Sentence Embeddings using Siamese BERT-Networks
abstract: We present Sentence-BERT, a modification of BERT using siamese and triplet network structures to derive semantically meaningful sentence embeddings for efficient similarity comparison.

title: MultiFC: A Real-World Multi-Domain Dataset for Evidence-Based Fact Checking of Claims
abstract: We present MultiFC, the largest publicly available dataset for fact checking, covering 36,534 claims from 26 fact checking websites across multiple domains.

title: Identifying and Attacking the Saddle Point Problem in High-Dimensional Non-Convex Optimization
abstract: We argue that saddle points rather than local minima are the primary obstacle for optimization in high-dimensional non-convex problems, and propose methods to escape them.

title: Data-centric Artificial Intelligence: A Survey
abstract: We provide a comprehensive survey of data-centric AI, covering data quality, data augmentation, and data-efficient learning approaches that focus on improving datasets rather than models.

title: Learning Safe Multi-Agent Control with Decentralized Neural Barrier Certificates
abstract: We propose a decentralized framework using neural barrier certificates for safe multi-agent control, ensuring collision avoidance while achieving cooperative objectives.

title: PyTorch: An Imperative Style, High-Performance Deep Learning Library
abstract: We present PyTorch, an imperative deep learning framework that provides GPU-accelerated tensor computation and automatic differentiation with a tape-based system.

title: TensorFlow: A System for Large-Scale Machine Learning
abstract: We describe TensorFlow, a machine learning system that operates at large scale and in heterogeneous environments using dataflow graphs to represent computation, shared state, and mutation operations.

title: FlashAttention: Fast and Memory-Efficient Exact Attention with IO-Awareness
abstract: We propose FlashAttention, an IO-aware exact attention algorithm that uses tiling to reduce memory reads/writes between GPU high bandwidth memory and on-chip SRAM, achieving 2-4x wall-clock speedup.

title: FlashAttention-2: Faster Attention with Better Parallelism and Work Partitioning
abstract: We propose FlashAttention-2, which improves upon FlashAttention with better work partitioning and parallelism to achieve up to 2x speedup, reaching 50-73% of theoretical maximum FLOPs on A100 GPUs.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
abstract: We combine pre-trained parametric and non-parametric memory for language generation, using a retrieval component to access Wikipedia and augment generation for knowledge-intensive tasks.

title: LLM.int8(): 8-bit Matrix Multiplication for Transformers at Scale
abstract: We develop a procedure for Int8 matrix multiplication for feed-forward and attention projection layers in transformers, enabling inference of models with up to 175B parameters without degradation.

title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
abstract: We propose LoRA, which freezes pre-trained model weights and injects trainable rank decomposition matrices into each Transformer layer, reducing trainable parameters by 10,000x and GPU memory by 3x.

title: XGBoost: A Scalable Tree Boosting System
abstract: We describe XGBoost, a scalable end-to-end tree boosting system that is used widely by data scientists with results on many machine learning challenges.

title: Billion-scale similarity search with GPUs
abstract: We present FAISS, a library for efficient similarity search and clustering of dense vectors, designed to handle billion-scale datasets using GPU acceleration.

title: Efficient Memory Management for Large Language Model Serving with PagedAttention
abstract: We propose PagedAttention, an attention algorithm inspired by virtual memory that allows KV cache memory to be stored in non-contiguous blocks, enabling near-zero waste in LLM serving.

title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
abstract: We introduce LLaMA, a collection of foundation language models ranging from 7B to 65B parameters trained on publicly available data, competitive with the best existing LLMs.

title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
abstract: We propose ReAct, a paradigm that prompts LLMs to generate both verbal reasoning traces and task-specific actions in an interleaved manner, enabling synergy between reasoning and acting.

title: Proximal Policy Optimization Algorithms
abstract: We propose PPO, a family of policy gradient methods that alternate between sampling data through interaction with the environment and optimizing a clipped surrogate objective function.

title: Self-attention Does Not Need O(n²) Memory
abstract: We show that self-attention can be computed with O(1) memory by leveraging the associativity of the softmax operation, enabling memory-efficient exact attention computation.

title: Neural Machine Translation of Rare Words with Subword Units
abstract: We introduce a simple method to represent open-vocabulary through byte pair encoding (BPE), allowing neural machine translation models to handle rare and unseen words via subword units.

title: Triton: An Intermediate Language and Compiler for Tiled Neural Network Computations
abstract: We present Triton, a language and compiler centered around the concept of tile, enabling researchers to write highly efficient GPU code for neural network computations with minimal effort.

title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
abstract: We propose Constitutional AI (CAI), a method for training AI systems to be helpful and harmless using AI feedback guided by a set of principles, reducing reliance on human feedback for harmlessness.

title: Efficient and robust approximate nearest neighbor search using Hierarchical Navigable Small World graphs
abstract: We propose HNSW, a graph-based approximate nearest neighbor search algorithm that builds a multi-layer structure of navigable small world graphs for logarithmic search complexity.

title: Milvus: A Purpose-Built Vector Data Management System
abstract: We present Milvus, a purpose-built vector data management system designed to manage and query billion-scale vectors with heterogeneous computing for approximate nearest neighbor search.

title: LightGBM: A Highly Efficient Gradient Boosting Decision Tree
abstract: We propose LightGBM, a gradient boosting framework that uses novel techniques of Gradient-based One-Side Sampling and Exclusive Feature Bundling to handle large-scale data efficiently.

title: Scikit-learn: Machine Learning in Python
abstract: We describe Scikit-learn, a Python module integrating a wide range of state-of-the-art machine learning algorithms for medium-scale supervised and unsupervised problems.
